text:
MEDICAL ONCOLOGY CLINIC NOTE
Department of Medical Oncology — Genitourinary Oncology Program

PATIENT: Margaret Okonkwo
ATTENDING PHYSICIAN: [Attending Medical Oncologist]
VISIT TYPE: Established Patient Follow-Up — Systemic Therapy Monitoring
DIAGNOSIS: Stage IV clear cell renal cell carcinoma (ccRCC), right kidney, with sarcomatoid differentiation (20%), pT3b pN1 M1 (bilateral pulmonary metastases, retroperitoneal lymphadenopathy), IMDC intermediate risk

---

CHIEF COMPLAINT

Mrs. Okonkwo is a 63-year-old Nigerian-American woman with metastatic clear cell RCC, currently receiving cabozantinib as second-line therapy, presenting for her scheduled 6-month cabozantinib monitoring visit and restaging assessment. She is approximately 15 months from her original diagnosis.

---

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS

Mrs. Okonkwo was originally diagnosed with stage IV ccRCC approximately 15 months ago, presenting with a large right renal mass (14.2 cm, T3b with renal vein tumor thrombus), a single right hilar retroperitoneal lymph node metastasis (2.4 cm), and bilateral pulmonary nodules (dominant right lower lobe nodule 1.8 cm), with additional subcentimeter nodules bilaterally. Tumor harbored 20% sarcomatoid differentiation and Fuhrman grade 4 histology. Molecular profiling revealed VHL and PBRM1 loss-of-function mutations, SETD2 frameshift, TP53 p.R175H, and notably MET high-level amplification. PD-L1 CPS was 12. She underwent cytoreductive right radical nephrectomy with retroperitoneal lymph node dissection, achieving R0 resection. Postoperatively she developed a transient ileus (Grade 2, resolved by POD 8) and mild anemia requiring iron supplementation.

Approximately 6 weeks post-nephrectomy, she initiated first-line systemic therapy with ipi 1 mg/kg plus Opdivo 3 mg/kg IV every 3 weeks (CheckMate 214 regimen), motivated by her IMDC intermediate-risk classification, sarcomatoid histology, and PD-L1 positivity. After cycle 2, she developed Grade 2 immune-related colitis requiring ipi discontinuation and an 8-week prednisone taper; Opdivo was continued as maintenance. She also developed subclinical immune-related hypothyroidism (TSH elevation with low-normal free T4), for which levothyroxine was initiated. First restaging at 3 months showed a mixed response pattern — the dominant right lower lobe nodule increased modestly (pseudoprogression vs. true progression debated), while other target lesions shrank. A confirmatory scan at 6 months, however, demonstrated unambiguous progression of the dominant lesion, two new right middle lobe pulmonary nodules, and a new right paracaval lymph node — confirming true progressive disease.

nivo was discontinued and second-line cabozantinib 60 mg orally once daily was initiated approximately 9 months after her initial systemic therapy start. Given her MET high-level amplification on NGS, cabozantinib (a dual VEGFR2/MET inhibitor) was selected as the preferred TKI. Early in the cabozantinib course, she experienced Grade 2 hand-foot skin reaction (HFSR), requiring dose reduction to 40 mg daily, with subsequent improvement to Grade 1. She also developed Grade 2 diarrhea (managed with loperamide and low-residue diet, resolving to Grade 1) and Grade 2 hypertension (amlodipine uptitrated to 10 mg daily with adequate BP control).

At her 3-month cabozantinib restaging assessment, CT chest/abdomen/pelvis demonstrated stable disease with an approximately 14% reduction in the sum of target lesion diameters from cabozantinib baseline. Today's visit is the 6-month cabozantinib assessment, coinciding with another restaging CT.

Since last visit, she reports overall feeling well. Energy is improved compared to the early months of cabozantinib and she is back to light gardening and household activities. She denies new or worsening HFSR symptoms — feet feel dry but not painful, and she has been consistently applying her urea cream. She reports occasional loose stools (1-2 per day above baseline), well-controlled with as-needed loperamide. She has been monitoring her blood pressure at home and values have consistently run between 130-140 mmHg systolic on her home cuff. She denies dyspnea, hemoptysis, cough, chest pain, or new musculoskeletal complaints. No new bone pain. No headaches, visual changes, or neurologic symptoms. No significant nausea or vomiting. Appetite is improved from nadir and she has regained approximately 2-3 lbs since last visit. She is sleeping well. She has been compliant with her cabozantinib and all supportive medications.

---

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS

Constitutional: No new weight loss; reports 2-3 lb weight gain since last visit. Fatigue Grade 1 at most, improved from prior. No fevers, no night sweats.
Respiratory: No dyspnea at rest or with exertion beyond mild baseline. No cough, no hemoptysis.
Cardiovascular: Home blood pressure 130-140/80-88 mmHg. No chest pain, palpitations, or edema.
Gastrointestinal: Ongoing intermittent loose stools 1-2 per day above baseline, controlled with PRN loperamide. No blood in stool. No nausea, vomiting, or abdominal pain. No dysgeusia significantly impairing oral intake.
Genitourinary: Solitary left kidney. No dysuria, hematuria. Creatinine stable per recent labs.
Musculoskeletal: No new bone pain. Prior low back discomfort substantially improved with physical therapy (naproxen use discontinued approximately 4 weeks ago). Mild hyperkeratosis of bilateral palms and soles — non-tender.
Dermatologic: Mild HFSR (Grade 1) only. No new rashes. No mucositis.
Neurologic: No new headaches, seizures, visual changes, focal weakness, or sensory deficits.
Endocrine: No symptoms of thyroid dysfunction. On levothyroxine.
Psychiatric: Mood reported as good. No depression or anxiety concerns raised.

---

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION

Vital Signs: Blood pressure 134/80 mmHg, heart rate 72 bpm (regular), respiratory rate 16 breaths/min, temperature 36.8°C, oxygen saturation 98% on room air, weight 78.1 kg (BMI 29.6).

General: Well-appearing Nigerian-American woman, in no acute distress. Alert, oriented, and engaged in discussion. Well-nourished.

Head, Eyes, Ears, Nose, Throat: Anicteric sclerae. Mucous membranes moist. No oral mucositis. No lymphadenopathy in the cervical, submandibular, or supraclavicular regions.

Cardiovascular: Regular rate and rhythm. Normal S1 and S2. No murmurs, rubs, or gallops. No peripheral edema.

Respiratory: Clear to auscultation bilaterally. No wheezes, crackles, or rhonchi. No dullness to percussion.

Abdomen: Soft, non-tender, non-distended. Right flank well-healed surgical scar (right radical nephrectomy). No palpable masses. No hepatosplenomegaly. No tenderness at the right nephrectomy bed.

Musculoskeletal: No point tenderness over the lumbar spine or paraspinal muscles on palpation. Full range of motion of the lumbar spine. No costovertebral angle tenderness.

Extremities: Bilateral palms and plantar surfaces of feet: mild hyperkeratosis, diffuse, predominantly over weight-bearing areas and lateral borders of the feet. Skin is thickened but not erythematous, blistered, or tender to touch (consistent with Grade 1 HFSR). Appropriate skin care regimen in place (urea 40% cream applied bilaterally). No digital ischemia.

Neurologic: Alert and oriented x3. Cranial nerves II-XII grossly intact. Motor strength 5/5 throughout. Sensation intact. Gait normal, steady.

Lymph Nodes: No palpable peripheral lymphadenopathy (cervical, axillary, inguinal).

---

LABORATORY RESULTS

Complete Blood Count:
- WBC: 5.8 x10^9/L (normal); ANC 3.4 x10^9/L
- Hemoglobin: 10.4 g/dL (Grade 1 anemia, mild, stable compared to prior)
- MCV: 88 fL (normocytic)
- Platelets: 204 x10^9/L (normal)

Comprehensive Metabolic Panel:
- Sodium 139 mEq/L, Potassium 4.1 mEq/L, Chloride 102 mEq/L, Bicarbonate 24 mEq/L
- BUN 22 mg/dL, Creatinine 1.4 mg/dL (eGFR approximately 41 mL/min/1.73m2; stable, expected in setting of solitary kidney and CKD stage 3b)
- Glucose 112 mg/dL (mildly elevated; known T2DM on metformin — within acceptable range)
- ALT 28 U/L (normal), AST 24 U/L (normal), Alkaline Phosphatase 78 U/L (normal), Total Bilirubin 0.7 mg/dL — no hepatotoxicity
- Albumin 3.9 g/dL
- LDH 202 U/L (mildly above upper limit of normal at 190 U/L; similar to prior)
- Corrected calcium 9.3 mg/dL (normal)

Urinalysis: Spot urine protein-to-creatinine ratio 0.18 (no clinically significant proteinuria; within acceptable range for VEGFR inhibitor monitoring).

Thyroid Function: TSH 3.8 mIU/L (euthyroid range; appropriate on levothyroxine 75 mcg daily — dose was up-titrated from 50 mcg at approximately the 4-month mark).

Lipid Panel (fasting): Total cholesterol 198 mg/dL, LDL 118 mg/dL, HDL 52 mg/dL, TG 132 mg/dL — within acceptable limits.

---

IMAGING RESULTS

Restaging CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with IV contrast was performed and reviewed in clinic today:

Pulmonary: The dominant right lower lobe target lesion measures 2.1 cm, down from 2.3 cm at the 3-month cabozantinib scan and 2.6 cm at cabozantinib initiation — continuing its slow but meaningful trajectory toward reduction. The second right lower lobe nodule is now 0.4 cm (from 0.5 cm at last scan), and the left lower lobe lesion measures 0.4 cm (stable). The two right middle lobe nodules previously identified as new disease at the time of progression on nivo have shrunk further to 0.4 cm and 0.3 cm. No new pulmonary nodules are identified. No pleural effusion.

Retroperitoneal/Lymph Nodes: The right paracaval lymph node, which measured 1.1 cm at cabozantinib initiation and had already decreased to 0.6 cm on the 3-month scan, is no longer discretely measurable on today's imaging — essentially a near-complete response of that nodal metastasis.

Abdomen/Pelvis: No new hepatic lesions. No adrenal lesions. Status post right nephrectomy with expected postoperative changes. No local recurrence at the nephrectomy bed. No peritoneal or mesenteric disease.

Osseous structures: No lytic or sclerotic bone lesions. Prior MRI lumbar spine (obtained several weeks ago for new back pain) confirmed only degenerative disc disease at L4-L5 without metastatic disease, consistent with today's scan.

Brain: MRI brain with gadolinium was performed concurrently. No evidence of intracranial metastatic disease. No leptomeningeal enhancement. No new findings.

Overall assessment: Continued favorable disease course. Sum of measurable target lesions is trending down — approximately 22% reduction from the cabozantinib baseline of 3.7 cm to 2.9 cm today. No new sites of disease. Near-complete nodal response. The pulmonary metastases continue slow but directionally consistent shrinkage.

---

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN

1. Stage IV clear cell renal cell carcinoma with sarcomatoid differentiation — STABLE DISEASE on cabozantinib, approximately 6 months of therapy, with continued meaningful tumor reduction across all sites of disease.

Mrs. Okonkwo continues to demonstrate a meaningful and durable response to cabozantinib 40 mg daily, now approximately 6 months into second-line therapy (cumulative roughly 15 months from diagnosis). The disease trajectory is encouraging: the dominant pulmonary target lesion has decreased from 2.6 cm at cabozantinib start to 2.1 cm today, representing a 19% further reduction at this site, and overall the sum of measurable disease has dropped approximately 22% from the cabozantinib baseline, edging closer to formal partial response territory (which would require a 30% reduction). The near-complete resolution of the right paracaval lymph node is a particularly gratifying finding. There is no evidence of new sites of metastatic disease, including on dedicated brain MRI. Given this favorable trajectory and her good performance status (ECOG 1), I plan to continue cabozantinib at the current 40 mg dose. The patient has previously deferred re-escalation to 60 mg given her preference to maintain current quality of life, and given that Grade 1 HFSR persists (although minimally symptomatic), I agree that dose escalation is not warranted at this time.

The MET high-level amplification on her tumor's NGS profile remains a biologically interesting feature, and cabozantinib's dual VEGFR2/MET inhibition continues to be a scientifically rational basis for her ongoing response. Whether the near-complete nodal response and more modest pulmonary reduction reflects differential MET dependence across disease sites is speculative but clinically interesting.

Plan: Continue cabozantinib 40 mg orally once daily. Next restaging CT chest/abdomen/pelvis in 3 months. Repeat MRI brain every 6 months given ccRCC's propensity for late CNS metastases. Ongoing monitoring for disease progression.

2. Hand-foot skin reaction (HFSR), Grade 1 — stable, well-managed.
Continue urea 40% cream applied twice daily to both hands and feet. Reinforced wearing well-cushioned, properly-fitting footwear and avoidance of prolonged ambulation on hard surfaces. No dose change warranted. Dermatology has previously been involved; no further dermatology referral needed at this time. I will plan to offer dose re-escalation to 60 mg if and when HFSR fully resolves.

3. TKI-related diarrhea, Grade 1 — intermittent, manageable.
Continue as-needed loperamide. Low-residue diet counseling reinforced. No dose change required. Patient tolerating well.

4. Hypertension — well-controlled on amlodipine 10 mg daily.
BP 134/80 mmHg in clinic today; home readings in the 130-140/80-88 mmHg range. Continue amlodipine 10 mg. Continue home blood pressure monitoring twice daily. No further antihypertensive adjustments needed at this time.

5. Immune-related hypothyroidism — euthyroid on levothyroxine 75 mcg daily.
TSH 3.8 mIU/L — in the appropriate range. Continue levothyroxine 75 mcg daily. Recheck TFTs in 3 months at next visit.

6. Chronic kidney disease (CKD stage 3b, solitary kidney post-nephrectomy) — stable.
Creatinine 1.4 mg/dL, eGFR approximately 41 mL/min/1.73m2, stable over multiple assessments. Urine protein-to-creatinine ratio 0.18 — no significant proteinuria. Continue metformin cautiously (dose adjusted given renal function). Continue monitoring creatinine at each visit. Avoid nephrotoxic agents. Adequate hydration reinforced, particularly given ongoing intermittent diarrhea and the warm season.

7. Type 2 diabetes mellitus — reasonably controlled.
Fasting glucose 112 mg/dL. Continue metformin at current dose. Consider endocrinology consultation if glycemic control worsens on ongoing steroid or future medication changes, but not required at this time.

8. Lumbar degenerative disc disease — improved.
Patient's low back pain has resolved substantially with physical therapy and a course of naproxen (now discontinued). MRI lumbar spine confirmed no osseous metastases. No bone-modifying agent indicated at this time — no skeletal involvement. Physical therapy exercises encouraged for ongoing back maintenance.

9. Anemia (Grade 1, normocytic, chronic) — stable.
Hemoglobin 10.4 g/dL, essentially unchanged from prior visits. Likely multifactorial: chronic disease anemia from ongoing malignancy, possible component from TKI-related suppression. No iron deficiency suggested (MCV normal, patient previously supplemented). No erythropoiesis-stimulating agent indicated at this time. Continue to monitor.

10. Quality of life.
Patient reports improved overall quality of life compared to the earlier cabozantinib period. Functional status ECOG 1 — independently performs ADLs, returned to light gardening. FKSI-DRS scores trending favorably. Psychosocial: Patient is emotionally well-adjusted and optimistic. She was previously a nurse and is highly health-literate; she asks excellent questions and engages actively in shared decision-making. We discussed the overall arc of her disease — approximately 15 months into a stage IV diagnosis, with two lines of therapy, and current stable disease — and I emphasized that this represents a genuinely favorable outcome relative to the expected natural history of sarcomatoid ccRCC. Continued support from her family noted.

Return to clinic in approximately 3 months (or sooner if new symptoms arise, particularly new bone pain, hemoptysis, neurologic symptoms, or HFSR flare). Labs to be drawn 1 week prior to next visit: CBC, CMP, LFTs, TSH, spot urine PCR.

Time spent in direct patient care and coordination: approximately 50 minutes.

[Electronically signed — Attending Medical Oncologist, Genitourinary Oncology Program]
